name: Event De-duplication System
scenario: You're building the event processing pipeline for a real-time system (like Razorpay's payment events or Zomato's order events). Events arrive from multiple sources and can be DUPLICATED — the same event sent twice due to network retries. Your system must process each event EXACTLY ONCE, track which events have been processed, and detect replay attacks. This is a real production concern: duplicate payment processing can charge a customer twice. HashSet is the solution.
description: Build an event deduplication pipeline using HashSet. Process events exactly once, detect duplicates, track processed event IDs, and implement a 'sliding window' where old event IDs are periodically cleared to prevent memory growth.

core.description: `Event` class: eventId (String, unique), type (String), payload (String), timestamp (long). Override equals and hashCode by eventId.
`EventProcessor` class: `Set<String>` processedIds. `process(Event e)` — if eventId not seen, process it (print) and add to seen; if already seen, print 'Duplicate ignored: eventId'.
`processAll(List<Event> events)` — process all events in order, returns count of unique events processed.
`getStats()` — prints: total attempted, unique processed, duplicates rejected.
main() demonstrates: 8 events with some duplicates (same eventId arriving multiple times), print stats.
Use Set<String> NOT Set<Event> for processed IDs (store just the ID string, not the whole event).
`findDuplicateIds(List<Event> events)` static method — returns Set<String> of eventIds that appear more than once in the list.

bonus.description: Add `groupByType(List<Event> events)` — returns Map<String, List<Event>> with events grouped by type (using computeIfAbsent from T6.2). De-duplicate within each group.
Add `reset(int keepLatestN)` — keeps only the N most recently processed eventIds in the Set, clearing older ones (use a LinkedHashSet to maintain insertion order for this).
Add `processedTypes()` — returns Set<String> of all unique event types that have been processed.

data_to_use:
Events (8 total with 3 duplicates):
- EVT-001, PAYMENT, order #101, ts=1000
- EVT-002, SIGNUP, user aisha@x.com, ts=1001
- EVT-001, PAYMENT, order #101, ts=1002  ← duplicate
- EVT-003, LOGIN, user aisha@x.com, ts=1003
- EVT-002, SIGNUP, user aisha@x.com, ts=1004  ← duplicate
- EVT-004, PAYMENT, order #102, ts=1005
- EVT-001, PAYMENT, order #101, ts=1006  ← duplicate (3rd time)
- EVT-005, LOGOUT, user aisha@x.com, ts=1007

Expected:
- 5 unique events processed
- 3 duplicates rejected
example_output:
===================================
     EVENT PROCESSING PIPELINE
===================================
Processed: EVT-001 (PAYMENT)
Processed: EVT-002 (SIGNUP)
Duplicate ignored: EVT-001
Processed: EVT-003 (LOGIN)
Duplicate ignored: EVT-002
Processed: EVT-004 (PAYMENT)
Duplicate ignored: EVT-001
Processed: EVT-005 (LOGOUT)

--- Stats ---
Attempted: 8
Processed (unique): 5
Duplicates rejected: 3

--- Duplicate IDs in batch ---
[EVT-001, EVT-002]

criteria: Set<String> not Set<Event> for IDs (5 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key challenge: findDuplicateIds needs to track which eventIds appear more than once — use two Sets (seen and duplicates), same pattern as exercise 5.
submission_instructions: Save as EventPipeline.java. Run it. Push to GitHub — this is real payment/order event deduplication architecture used by Razorpay, Stripe, and every event-driven system.
tips_for_success: EventProcessor.process(): add eventId to processedIds. add() returns true if new, false if duplicate.
Track totals: attempted++ always, processed++ when add() returns true, duplicates++ when false.
findDuplicateIds: use the seen/duplicates two-set pattern from exercise 5.
bonus reset(): LinkedHashSet preserves insertion order — iterate, keep last N, clear, re-add the N.
concepts_reinforced: add() return value (true = new, false = duplicate)
Set<String> storing IDs (not full objects) — efficient and clear
